hero.title: Digital India Election Process
hero.subtitle: Understand voting, elections, and democracy in a simple interactive way.

intro.title: What are Elections?
intro.desc1: Elections are the foundation of a democratic society. It is the process by which citizens choose their leaders and representatives.
intro.desc2: By voting, you actively participate in deciding the future of the country and ensuring your voice is heard.

voice.title: Equal Voice
voice.desc: Every citizen's vote carries equal weight, regardless of background.

democracy.title: Strengthens Democracy
democracy.desc: High voter participation builds a stronger, more representative government.

responsibility.title: Civic Duty
responsibility.desc: Voting is both a fundamental right and a core responsibility.

announcement.title: Election Announcement
announcement.desc: The Election Commission of India announces the schedule.

campaign.title: Campaign Period
campaign.desc: Candidates present their vision to the voters.

verification.title: Voter Verification
verification.desc: Citizens check their names on the electoral roll.

voting.title: Voting Day
voting.desc: Voters cast their ballots securely using EVMs.

counting.title: Vote Counting
counting.desc: Votes are counted under strict supervision.

results.title: Election Results
results.desc: Winners are declared and the new government forms.

loksabha.title: Lok Sabha Election
loksabha.desc: General elections to elect Members of Parliament (MPs) to the lower house, who in turn select the Prime Minister.

rajyasabha.title: Rajya Sabha Election
rajyasabha.desc: Indirect elections where Members of Legislative Assemblies (MLAs) elect representatives to the upper house.

assembly.title: State Assembly Election
assembly.desc: Elections to elect MLAs who form the state government and choose the Chief Minister.

local.title: Local Body Elections
local.desc: Elections for municipalities, panchayats, and local councils to manage local governance.

loksabha.title: House of the People (Lower House)
loksabha.method: Citizens directly elect members through general elections, giving them a direct voice in national decisions.
loksabha.term: Normally has a term of 5 years.
loksabha.role: Plays an important role in forming the central government (the Prime Minister is usually from here). Mainly handles public issues, financial matters, and government accountability.

rajyasabha.title: Council of States (Upper House)
rajyasabha.rep: Represents the states of India, helping protect the interests of states in Parliament.
rajyasabha.method: Members are chosen indirectly by elected state representatives (MLAs).
rajyasabha.term: Members serve for 6 years. It is a permanent house and is never fully dissolved.
rajyasabha.role: Reviews bills/laws passed by Lok Sabha, discusses national issues, and provides stability in the law-making process.

items.address: Ordinary Resident of the polling area

fakenews.title: Avoid Fake News
fakenews.desc: Verify information before sharing it with others.

verify.title: Verify Information
verify.desc: Always check official sources like the Election Commission website.

respect.title: Respect Democracy
respect.desc: Respect the electoral process and the right of others to vote.

responsible.title: Vote Responsibly
responsible.desc: Make an informed decision based on facts and candidate history.

1.q: How to register for a Voter ID?
1.a: You can register online through the Voter's Service Portal (voters.eci.gov.in) or offline by filling Form 6 at your local Electoral Registration Office.

2.a: EVM stands for Electronic Voting Machine. It is an electronic device used for recording votes securely and reliably.

3.q: Can I vote without a Voter ID card?
3.a: If your name is on the electoral roll, you can vote using alternate approved photo identity documents like Aadhaar, PAN Card, Driving License, etc.

4.q: What documents are required for registration?
4.a: You need a passport-sized photograph, an age proof (like birth certificate or Aadhaar), and an address proof (like electricity bill or passport).

5.a: Votes recorded in EVMs are counted electronically in the presence of candidates or their agents on a specified counting day under strict security.

footer.disclaimer: This platform is purely for educational purposes and is politically neutral.
